heading: Limitations on substitution of unprocessed Federal timber for unprocessed timber exported from private lands
label: 620b Limitations on substitution of unprocessed Federal timber for unprocessed timber exported from private lands

children.label: A
children.text: such unprocessed timber is to be used in substitution for exported unprocessed timber originating from private lands; or

children.label: B
children.text: such person has, during the preceding 24-month period, exported unprocessed timber originating from private lands.

children.label: A
children.text: Federal timber purchased pursuant to a contract entered into between the purchaser and the Secretary concerned before the date on which regulations to carry out this subsection are issued under shall be governed by the regulations of the Secretary concerned in effect before such date that restrict the substitution of unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands for exported timber originating from private lands;section 620f of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the 1-year period beginning on , any person who operates under a Cooperative Sustained Yield Unit Agreement, and who has an historic export quota shall be limited to entering into contracts under such a quota to a volume equal to not more than 66 percent of the person’s historic export quota used during fiscal year 1989;August 20, 1990

children.label: C
children.text: a person referred to in subparagraph (B) shall reduce the person’s remaining substitution volume by an equal amount each year thereafter such that no volume is substituted under such a quota in fiscal year 1995 or thereafter; and

children.label: i
children.text: before , has, under an historic export quota approved by the Secretary concerned, purchased unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands west of the 100th meridian in the contiguous 48 States in substitution for exported unprocessed timber originating from private lands;August 20, 1990

children.label: ii
children.text: certifies to the Secretary concerned, within 3 months after , that the person will, within 6 months after , cease exporting unprocessed timber originating from private lands; andAugust 20, 1990August 20, 1990

children.label: iii
children.text: ceases exports in accordance with such certification.

children.label: A
children.text: the private lands referred to in paragraph (1) are owned by the person; or

children.label: B
children.text: the person has the exclusive right to harvest timber from the private lands described in paragraph (1) during a period of more than 7 years, and may exercise that right at any time of the person’s choosing.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), no person may, beginning 21 days after , purchase from any other person unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands west of the 100th meridian in the contiguous 48 States if such person would be prohibited from purchasing such timber directly from a department or agency of the United States. Acquisitions of western red cedar which are domestically processed into finished products to be sold into domestic or international markets are exempt from the prohibition contained in this paragraph.August 20, 1990

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of such timber acquired by such person, based on the higher of the applicant’s actual timber purchasing receipts or the appropriate Federal agency’s records, during fiscal years 1988, 1989, and 1990, divided by 3, or

children.label: ii
children.text: 15 million board feet,

children.label: B
children.text: The Federal lands referred to in subparagraph (A) are Federal lands administered by the United States Forest Service Region 6 that are located north of the Columbia River from its mouth and east to its first intersection with the 119th meridian, and from that point north of the 46th parallel and east.

children.label: C
children.text: Any person may sell, trade, or otherwise exchange with any other person the rights obtained under subparagraph (A), except that such rights may not be sold, traded, or otherwise exchanged to persons already in possession of such rights obtained under subparagraph (A).

children.label: D
children.text: Federal timber purchased from Federal lands described in subparagraph (B) pursuant to a contract entered into between the purchaser and the Secretary of Agriculture before the date on which regulations to carry out this subsection are issued under shall be governed by the regulations of the Secretary of Agriculture in effect before such date that restrict the substitution of unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands for exported timber originating from private lands.section 620f of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the previous 24 months, has not exported unprocessed timber originating from private lands within the sourcing area; and

children.label: B
children.text: during the period in which such approval is in effect, does not export unprocessed timber originating from private lands within the sourcing area.

children.heading: Requirements for application for sourcing areas for processing facilities located outside the northwestern private timber open market area
children.label: 2 Requirements for application for sourcing areas for processing facilities located outside the northwestern private timber open market area
A
children.text: information regarding the location of private lands (except private land located in the northwestern private timber open market area) from which such person has, within the previous year, harvested or otherwise acquired unprocessed timber which has been exported from the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: information regarding the location of each timber manufacturing facility owned or operated by such person within the proposed sourcing area boundaries at which the applicant proposes to process timber originating from Federal lands.

children.heading: Grant of approval for sourcing areas for processing facilities located outside of the northwestern private timber open market area
children.label: 3 Grant of approval for sourcing areas for processing facilities located outside of the northwestern private timber open market area

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For each applicant, the Secretary concerned shall, on the record and after an opportunity for a hearing, not later than 4 months after receipt of the application for a sourcing area, either approve or disapprove the application. The Secretary concerned may approve such application only if the Secretary determines that the area that is the subject of the application, in which the timber manufacturing facilities at which the applicant desires to process timber originating from Federal lands are located, is geographically and economically separate from any geographic area from which that person harvests for export any unprocessed timber originating from private lands.

children.heading: For timber manufacturing facilities located in Idaho
children.label: B For timber manufacturing facilities located in Idaho
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (D), in making a determination referred to in subparagraph (A), the Secretary concerned shall consider the private timber export and the private and Federal timber sourcing patterns for the applicant’s timber manufacturing facilities, as well as the private and Federal timber sourcing patterns for the timber manufacturing facilities of other persons in the same local vicinity of the applicant, and the relative similarity of such private and Federal timber sourcing patterns.

children.heading: For timber manufacturing facilities located in States other than Idaho
children.label: C For timber manufacturing facilities located in States other than Idaho
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (D), in making the determination referred to in subparagraph (A), the Secretary concerned shall consider the private timber export and the Federal timber sourcing patterns for the applicant’s timber manufacturing facilities, as well as the Federal timber sourcing patterns for the timber manufacturing facilities of other persons in the same local vicinity of the applicant, and the relative similarity of such Federal timber sourcing patterns. Private timber sourcing patterns shall not be a factor in such determinations in States other than Idaho.

children.label: i
children.text: consider land located in the northwestern private timber open market area; or

children.label: ii
children.text: condition approval of the application on the inclusion of any such land in the applicant’s sourcing area, such land being includable in the sourcing area only to the extent requested by the applicant.

children.heading: Denial of application for sourcing areas for processing facilities located outside the northwestern private timber open market area
children.label: 4 Denial of application for sourcing areas for processing facilities located outside the northwestern private timber open market area
A
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), and notwithstanding any other provision of law, in the 9-month period after receiving disapproval of an application submitted pursuant to this subsection, the applicant may purchase unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands in the area which is the subject of the application in an amount not to exceed 75 percent of the annual average of such person’s purchases of unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands in the same area during the 5 full fiscal years immediately prior to submission of the application. In the subsequent 6-month period, such person may purchase not more than 25 percent of such annual average, after which time the prohibitions contained in subsection (a) shall fully apply.

children.label: B
children.text: If a person referred to in subparagraph (A) certifies to the Secretary concerned, within 90 days after receiving disapproval of such application, that such person shall, within 15 months after such disapproval, cease the export of unprocessed timber originating from private lands from the geographic area determined by the Secretary for which the application would have been approved, such person may continue to purchase unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands in the area which is the subject of the application, without being subject to the restrictions of subparagraph (A), except that such purchases during that 15-month period may not exceed 125 percent of the annual average of such person’s purchases of unprocessed timber originating from Federal lands in the same area during the 5 full fiscal years immediately prior to submission of the application which was denied.

children.label: C
children.text: Any person to whom subparagraph (B) applies may not, during the 15-month period after the person’s application for sourcing area boundaries is denied, export unprocessed timber originating from private lands in the geographic area determined by the Secretary concerned for which the application would have been approved in amounts that exceed 125 percent of the annual average of such person’s exports of unprocessed timber from such private lands during the 5 full fiscal years immediately prior to submission of the application.

children.heading: Review of determinations for sourcing areas for processing facilities located outside the northwestern private timber open market area
children.label: 5 Review of determinations for sourcing areas for processing facilities located outside the northwestern private timber open market area
children.text: Determinations made under paragraph (3) shall be reviewed, in accordance with the procedures prescribed in sections 620 to 620j of this title, not less often than every 5 years.

children.heading: Sourcing areas for processing facilities located in the northwestern private timber open market area
children.label: 6 Sourcing areas for processing facilities located in the northwestern private timber open market area
i
children.text: a sourcing area boundary shall be a circle around the processing facility of the sourcing area applicant or holder;

children.label: I
children.text: shall be the furthest distance that the sourcing area applicant or holder proposes to haul Federal timber for processing at the processing facility; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall be determined solely by the sourcing area applicant or holder;

children.label: iii
children.text: a sourcing area shall become effective on written notice to the Regional Forester for Region 6 of the Forest Service of the location of the boundary of the sourcing area;

children.label: iv
children.text: the 24-month requirement in paragraph (1)(A) shall not apply;

children.label: I
children.text: may adjust the radius of the sourcing area not more frequently than once every 24 months; and

children.label: II
children.text: shall provide written notice to the Regional Forester for Region 6 of the adjusted boundary of its sourcing area before using the adjusted sourcing area; and

children.label: vi
children.text: a sourcing area holder that relinquishes a sourcing area may not reestablish a sourcing area for that processing facility before the date that is 24 months after the date on which the sourcing area was relinquished.

children.label: i
children.text: that portion shall continue to be a sourcing area only until unprocessed Federal timber from the portion is no longer in the possession of the sourcing area holder; and

children.label: ii
children.text: unprocessed timber from private land in that portion shall be exportable immediately after unprocessed timber from Federal land in the portion is no longer in the possession of the sourcing area holder.

children.heading: Relinquishment and termination of sourcing areas
children.label: 7 Relinquishment and termination of sourcing areas

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A sourcing area may be relinquished at any time.

children.heading: Effective date
children.label: B Effective date
children.text: A relinquishment of a sourcing area shall be effective as of the date on which written notice is provided by the sourcing area holder to the Regional Forester with jurisdiction over the sourcing area where the processing facility of the holder is located.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: On relinquishment or termination of a sourcing area, unprocessed timber from private land within the former boundary of the relinquished or terminated sourcing area is exportable immediately after unprocessed timber from Federal land from within that area is no longer in the possession of the former sourcing area holder.

children.heading: No restriction
children.label: ii No restriction
children.text: The exportability of unprocessed timber from private land located outside of a sourcing area shall not be restricted or in any way affected by relinquishment or termination of a sourcing area.

children.heading: Domestic transportation and processing of private timber
children.label: d Domestic transportation and processing of private timber
children.text: Nothing in this section restricts or authorizes any restriction on the domestic transportation or processing of timber harvested from private land, except that the Secretary may prohibit processing facilities located in the State of Idaho that have sourcing areas from processing timber harvested from private land outside of the boundaries of those sourcing areas.
